title: tocoyena costanensis subsp. andina
intro: Tocoyena costanensis subsp. andina, also known as the Andean Tocoyena or Tocoyena, is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tocoyena costanensis subsp. andina include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
